lines.en_lit: GEMARA: whom did you learn that he said between the Entrance Hall and the altar north

It is Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, as it was taught What is north? from the northern wall of the altar until the wall of the courtyard. And opposite the entire altar north; the statement of Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Yehuda. And Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, adds even between the Entrance Hall and the altar Rabbi adds even the place where the priests walk, and even the place where the Israelites walk, However, everyone agrees that from the of the knives and inward, is unfit
Let us say is Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, and not Rabbi Even you say Rabbi now, Rabbi add Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Yehuda, does not add Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon?
This is what we are saying: If Rabbi let us stand in the entire courtyard Rather, what is Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon? And let us stand between the altar and the wall,
Rather, what have you to say due to the weakness High Priest, Rabbi as well, due to the weakness High Priest
The head to the south and its face to the west. what circumstances be found Rav said: it turns its head And let us stand it straight Abaye said: It is a decree lest defecate

The Sages taught: How place his hands the offering? stands in the north and its face to the west, and the one who is placing his hands stands to the east and his face to the west, and he places his two hands between the two horns of offering, provided nothing interposes between his and the offering. And he confesses over a sin-offering, transgression sin-offering, And over a guilt-offering the transgression guilt-offering, And over a burnt-offering, the sin of gleanings, forgotten sheaves, and produce of the corners [pe’a], as well as poor man’s tithe. the statement of Rabbi Yosei HaGelili.
Rabbi Akiva says: A burnt-offering is brought only over a positive and over a prohibition that is transformed into a positive
With regard to what do they disagree? Rabbi Yirmeya said:
